info.title: laut.fm Radioadmin-Api
info.description: Dies ist eine nach bestem Wissen erstellte Dokumentation der laut.fm-Radioadmin-API. Wir werden sie auch in Zukunft aktualisieren, wenn wir Unregelmäßigkeiten feststellen oder neue Endpunkte hinzufügen bzw. bestehende Endpunkte verändern. Die bisherige Version der Dokumentation mit anklickbaren Links findet ihr immer noch

get.description: View the definition of a specific automation algorithm

put.description: Add an automation algorithm for shuffling tracks

patch.description: Update the function used when shuffling tracks

requestBody.description:
You may pass a body object containing one or more of the `key: value` pairs from the schema.

Note that in this example you should **delete** any `key: value` you do not want to update.

get.description: List of all users of a station

post.description: Add a user with a given role to a station

patch.description: Change the role of a user

delete.description: Remove a user from a station

get.description: List of all playlists for a station

post.description: Add a new playlist to a station

requestBody.description:
You must provide at least:
  - name
  - color (in a full hex digit format not the shortened version)

get.description: Get information for a single playlist for a station

patch.description: Modify an existing playlist for a station

post.description:
Append a single track to the playlist.

This endpoint will not allow the addition of tracks which are already present in the playlist.

If a track is appended to the currently scheduled playlist, the appended track will be buffered into the stream at the next available buffering opportunity.

delete.description: Delete a single playlist for a station. The rotation playlist can not be deleted

get.description: All tracks for a playlist. Note that tracks are unique and not (necessarily) in the playlist order

delete.description: Delete all occurences of the track from the playlist

get.description: get the station image with the given type

put.description:
Upload an image of a given type for a station

We accept JPEGs, PNGs and GIFs

patch.description: You can not add an entry for the Base Playlist into entries. Additionally, if you change the base playlist ID, any entries containing that ID will be deleted.

get.description: We have attempted to document and test this endpoint as well as possible; however, due to it's nature we can not guarantee that all parameters works as expected or that there are not parameters missing. This endpoint has been deprecated. It will be replaced in a future version.
Get information about when a track was played, how many listeners listened to it, as well as if it was sent live in addition to general track metadata of the current day
Get information about when a track was played, how many listeners listened to it, as well as if it was sent live in addition to general track metadata.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: allowed values are `24h` or a date in the form `yyyy-mm-dd`

get.description:
Listen to a portion of a track as cut by Laut FM for determining the quality and verify it is the track you want.
The Version with query strings is intended to be used for providing the prelisten _without_ having to authenticate to the radioadmin.
Indicates if the station is active and playing on a streaming server

post.description: Will trigger a playlist export (in all cases) and start a station on a streaming server if it is assigned to one and not currently running

used to generate a station name derived from a display name and to validate this station name may be used for as-you-type feedback to the user endpoint is only available to previously unknown app users

message.example: too short or not enough valid characters
daily station limit on server exceeded

post.description: used create a new station with a name derived from the given display name also creates a local FirebaseUser with the Firebase ID provided by the Firebase Token endpoint is only available to previously unknown app users

message.example: daily station limit on server exceeded
daily station limit on server exceeded

id.description:
This will be a negative integer indicating that it has not yet been processed.

This negative integer can be polled via the `/stations/{station_id}/tracks/{track_ids}` endpoint to determine when it has been processed.

example.message: Record not found. Note that this also could be a record where you don't have the access rights with this access token.
The user is not a member of this station or no valid membership given
